name: Cottage Ham'n' Apple Quiche
recipeIngredient: 4 raw refrigerated buttermilk biscuits (1 oz each)
1/2 pound apples, cored and pared, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
2. Carefully separate each biscuit into 4 thin layers of dough (do this as soon as you take it from the refrigerator).
3. In a 8 inch pie plate, arrange biscuits with edges touching, in concentric circles over bottom and up sides of the plate. Use fingers to spread the dough to form a crust, pressing edges together to seal.
4. Place a sheet of foil over the bottom of the dough and fill with uncooked dry beans. Bake for 5 minutes.
5. Remove beans and foil; set crust aside.
6. In a 10 inch nonstick skillet, heat margarine over medium-high heat until bubbly. Add apples, ham, and onion, and saute until apples are tender (2 to 3 minutes).
7. Spoon the apple mixture into the crust and sprinkle with shredded cheese; set aside.
8. In a blender container, combine Cottage cheese, eggs, milk, and seasonings. Process until smooth, scraping sides of the container as necessary.
9. Pour the mixture into the pie plate.
10. Set the pie plate on a baking sheet and bake for 30 to 40 minutes (until puffed and lightly browned and a knife inserted in the center comes out clean).
11. Remove from oven and keep warm, then let stand for 10 minutes before serving. Makes 4 servings.
